title: Building a Running Product
notes: There is no PRODUCT() window aggregate, so the product is built from a running SUM of logarithms: EXP(SUM(LN(x))) equals the product of the x values, because ln(a*b) = ln(a)+ln(b). EXP/LN reintroduce tiny floating error, so the result is ROUNDed to 2 decimals to recover the exact product. MSSQL has no LN(); reference.mssql.sql uses LOG(), which is the natural log in SQL Server.
